for dir in C:/Users/tjn/.claude/skills/*/; do
  if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
    folder_name=$(basename "$dir")

    echo "Folder: $folder_name | SKILL.md: $has_skill_md | README.md: $has_readme | Files: $file_count"
  fi
done | sort

# Claude Code Skills Library

Custom skills for Claude Code - reusable across any project.

**Version:** 2.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Last Updated:** December 2025

---

## Generic Skills (Use for Any Project)

### Development Skills

| Skill | Purpose | When to Use |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| **generic-code-reviewer** | Multi-stack code review | Before commits, PR reviews |
| **generic-feature-developer** | Architecture patterns | Adding features, planning changes |
| **generic-design-system** | Design tokens & patterns | UI implementation, styling |
| **generic-ux-designer** | UX best practices | Design reviews, user flows |

### Utility Skills

| Skill | Purpose | When to Use |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| **codebase-documenter** | Generate documentation | README, API docs, comments |
| **tech-debt-analyzer** | Code health analysis | Audits, refactoring planning |
| **test-specialist** | Testing guidance | Writing tests, debugging |
| **frontend-enhancer** | Frontend patterns | UI improvements |
| **document-skills** | Office docs (docx, xlsx, pptx, pdf) | Document creation |
| **seo-analytics-auditor** | SEO analysis | Performance audits |

---

## Example Skills (Customization Templates)

These skills demonstrate how to customize for specific project types. Copy and modify for your own projects.

### example-static-site-* (Static HTML/CSS/JS Sites)
- **example-static-site-code-reviewer** - Pure HTML/CSS/JS, performance focus
- **example-static-site-design-system** - Minimalist design patterns
- **example-static-site-feature-developer** - Landing page patterns
- **example-static-site-ux-designer** - Lightweight UX constraints

### example-react-app-* (React/TypeScript Applications)
- **example-react-app-code-reviewer** - React/TypeScript, privacy-first
- **example-react-app-design-system** - Dark mode, accessibility focus
- **example-react-app-feature-developer** - Local-first architecture
- **example-react-app-ux-designer** - Productivity app UX

### example-fullstack-* (Full-Stack Next.js/NestJS)
- **example-fullstack-code-reviewer** - Next.js + NestJS, Prisma
- **example-fullstack-design-system** - Modern theme patterns
- **example-fullstack-feature-developer** - Full-stack patterns
- **example-fullstack-ux-designer** - SaaS app UX

---

## How to Use Skills

### Automatic Activation
Skills activate when relevant context appears in conversation.

### Explicit Invocation
Request skills directly:
```
Use generic-code-reviewer to review this code
Use generic-design-system for the color choices
Use tech-debt-analyzer on this codebase
```

### Skill Tool
```
Skill(generic-code-reviewer)
Skill(tech-debt-analyzer)
```

---

## Skill Structure

Each skill follows this structure:

```
skills/
└── [skill-name]/
    ├── SKILL.md          # Main skill file (required)
    ├── references/       # Reference materials (optional)
    ├── assets/           # Templates, examples (optional)
    └── scripts/          # Helper scripts (optional)
```

### SKILL.md Format

```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: Brief description for when Claude should use this skill
---

# Skill Title

Content with guidance, rules, patterns, examples...
```

---

## Creating New Skills

### 1. Create Folder
```bash
mkdir -p .claude/skills/[skill-name]
```

### 2. Create SKILL.md
- YAML frontmatter with name and description
- Comprehensive content for Claude to follow
- Examples and patterns

### 3. Add References (Optional)
Place in `references/` folder for additional context.

### 4. Test
Verify skill activates when expected context appears.

---

## Skill Categories

### By Tech Stack

| Stack | Recommended Skills |
|-------|-------------------|
| HTML/CSS/JS | generic-*, example-static-site-* |
| React/TypeScript | generic-*, example-react-app-* |
| Next.js/NestJS | generic-*, example-fullstack-* |
| Python | generic-code-reviewer, tech-debt-analyzer |
| Any | codebase-documenter, test-specialist |

### By Task Type

| Task | Recommended Skills |
|------|-------------------|
| Code review | generic-code-reviewer, [project]-code-reviewer |
| New feature | generic-feature-developer |
| UI/Styling | generic-design-system |
| UX design | generic-ux-designer |
| Documentation | codebase-documenter |
| Code health | tech-debt-analyzer |
| Testing | test-specialist |
| Documents | document-skills |

---

## Skills vs Other Claude Code Features

| Feature | Use For |
|---------|---------|
| **Skills** | Domain knowledge, guidelines, patterns |
| **Slash Commands** | Quick actions, workflows |
| **Hooks** | Automated responses to events |
| **Subagents** | Complex multi-step tasks |
| **MCP Servers** | External tool integration |

---

## Best Practices

### When to Create a Skill
- Repeated guidance needed
- Project-specific standards
- Complex domain knowledge
- Reusable patterns

### When NOT to Create a Skill
- One-time instructions
- Simple preferences (use CLAUDE.md)
- External tool integration (use MCP)

### Skill Content Tips
- Be comprehensive but focused
- Include concrete examples
- Reference other skills when relevant
- Keep updated as project evolves

---

## See Also

- `.claude/docs/workflow-automation.md` - Workflow automation & checklists
- `.claude/skills/MASTER_INDEX.md` - Skills navigation hub
- `.claude/skills/FORTUNE-50-COMPLETE-GUIDE.md` - Enterprise skills guide
- `.claude/commands/` - Custom slash commands
- `.claude/templates/` - Task templates
- `CLAUDE.md` - Project workflow rules

---

## Questions?

- Skills not activating? Check skill name and description match context
- Need new skill? Follow existing patterns in this folder
- Skill conflicts? Review descriptions for proper use cases

---
name: generic-code-reviewer
description: Review code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, accessibility gaps, and CLAUDE.md workflow compliance. Supports any tech stack - HTML/CSS/JS, React, TypeScript, Node.js, Python, NestJS, Next.js, and more. Use when completing features, before commits, or reviewing pull requests.

## Skills Library Analysis - Comprehensive Report

Based on my in-depth exploration of C:/Users/tjn/.claude/skills/, here are my detailed findings:

## 1. TOTAL SKILL COUNT & STRUCTURE

**Total folders:** 42 (including utilities and special folders)

- 2 special folders (_shared, document-skills)

**Total size:** 4.7 MB (heavily influenced by document-skills at 2.9MB)

---

## 2. MISSING SKILL.MD FILES

**Status:** All skill folders except 2 have proper SKILL.md files:

**MISSING:**
- `document-skills/` - Contains only package.json, index.js, and bulky OOXML schema files. This appears to be a utility module (not a real skill) holding document processing references.
- `_shared/` - Contains shared reference files (CODE_REVIEW_STANDARDS.md, DESIGN_PATTERNS.md, UX_PRINCIPLES.md) but no SKILL.md. This is intentionally a reference folder, not a skill.

**Verdict:** No critical missing files. Both exceptions are intentional.

---

## 3. REDUNDANCY & OVERLAPPING SKILLS

### A. GENERIC SKILLS - Significant Hierarchy Problem

You have **16 generic skills** organized in a potentially confusing hierarchy:

| Category | Count | Skills |
|----------|-------|--------|
| **Universal** | 4 | generic-code-reviewer, generic-design-system, generic-feature-developer, generic-ux-designer |
| **Stack-Specific** | 12 | 4x static, 4x react, 4x fullstack (each with code-reviewer, design-system, feature-developer, ux-designer) |

**Problem:** Massive redundancy. The stack-specific skills largely duplicate generic skills with minor stack-specific tweaks.

**Example redundancy:**
- `generic-code-reviewer` (2K) - Multi-stack code review
- `generic-react-code-reviewer` (2K) - React-specific code review
- `generic-fullstack-code-reviewer` (2K) - Fullstack-specific code review
- `generic-static-code-reviewer` (2K) - Static site code review

All share identical structure, just different context. Same pattern repeats for design-system, feature-developer, and ux-designer.

**Recommendation:** See consolidation strategy below.

### B. ENTERPRISE vs FORTUNE50 - Content Duplication

**enterprise-marketing-strategy** (921 lines, 29KB) vs **fortune50-marketing** (455 lines, 11KB)
**enterprise-sales-excellence** (1400+ lines, 50KB) vs **fortune50-sales** (455 lines, 11KB)

**Problem:** enterprise-* skills are significantly longer and more detailed versions of fortune50-* equivalents. Both serve the same purpose.

**Verdict:** Redundant. Should consolidate into single Fortune 50 skill line or clearly differentiate the use cases.

---

## 4. INCONSISTENT FOLDER NAMING

**Issue:** README.md references "example-*" skills that don't exist.

Example from README.md (lines 38-54):
```
### example-static-site-* (Static HTML/CSS/JS Sites)
- example-static-site-code-reviewer
- example-static-site-design-system
...
### example-react-app-* (React/TypeScript Applications)

...
### example-fullstack-* (Full-Stack Next.js/NestJS)

```

**Reality:** These folders don't exist. They're named `generic-*` instead.

**Verdict:** Documentation is out of sync with actual implementation. Need to either:
1. Rename all `generic-*` to `example-*` (but then what are the universal ones?)
2. Update documentation to match actual folder names

---

## 5. LARGE REFERENCE FILES ANALYSIS

| Folder | Size | Main Files | Issue |
|--------|------|-----------|-------|
| document-skills | 2.9M | 2000+ XSD schema files | **Bloated** - Contains entire OOXML spec. Never used by Claude. |
| fortune50-finance | 220K | Single SKILL.md (11KB + reference docs) | Acceptable |
| fortune50-legal-compliance | 120K | Single SKILL.md | Acceptable |
| enterprise-sales-excellence | 76K | SKILL.md (50KB) | Large but justified (comprehensive methodology) |
| enterprise-marketing-strategy | 48K | SKILL.md (29KB) | Large but justified |
| codebase-documenter | 118K | SKILL.md + references | Acceptable (documentation utility) |

**Major Issue:** `document-skills/` at 2.9MB is 62% of the entire skills folder. It contains XSD schema definitions for Office formats that are never actually used. This can be safely removed or archived.

---

## 6. SKILLS WITHOUT ACTIVE USE OR POTENTIALLY OBSOLETE

Based on folder size/structure analysis:

**Likely Under-Utilized:**
- `seo-analytics-auditor` (8KB) - Single small SKILL.md, no supporting materials
- `tech-debt-analyzer` (70KB) - Has materials but niche use case
- `frontend-enhancer` (67KB) - Overlaps heavily with generic-react-ux-designer and generic-fullstack-ux-designer

**Candidates for Consolidation:**
- `document-skills` - Appears to be deprecated/placeholder (see #2 above)
- All `generic-static-*` skills - If you primarily work with React/Next.js, these may be unused

---

## 7. GENERIC-* VS EXAMPLE-* SKILLS - NAMING CONFUSION

**Current Reality:**
- You have `generic-*` skills (not `example-*`)
- Documentation promises `example-*` skills that don't exist
- README says these are "customization templates" but they're already deployed as skills

**What's Actually There:**
```
generic-code-reviewer              (universal - any tech stack)
generic-{react|static|fullstack}-code-reviewer  (stack-specific implementations)
```

**The Confusing Part:** 
The README (line 34-54) calls these "Example Skills" and says "Copy and modify for your own projects" but they're actually deployed as real skills in your skill library, not examples.

---

## 8. NAMING INCONSISTENCY PATTERNS

**Inconsistent Patterns:**
```
fortune50-*               (clear: Fortune 50 expert skills)
enterprise-*             (ambiguous: Are these Fortune 50? Different from fortune50-*?)
generic-*                (confusing: Generic what? Should be "stack-specific-*"?)
generic-react-*          (clearer: But inconsistent with "generic" prefix)
_shared                  (hidden convention with underscore - unclear)
```

**Better naming would be:**
```
generic-*                (truly universal: code-reviewer, design-system, feature-developer, ux-designer)
template-react-*         (stack-specific: template-react-code-reviewer)
template-fullstack-*     (stack-specific: template-fullstack-code-reviewer)
template-static-*        (stack-specific: template-static-code-reviewer)
fortune50-*              (enterprise expertise - keep as is)
```

---

## 9. SUMMARY TABLE

| Category | Count | Status | Issue | Priority |
|----------|-------|--------|-------|----------|
| **Skill Folders** | 40 | ✓ All have SKILL.md | N/A | - |
| **Generic Skills** | 16 | Redundant | 12/16 are stack-specific duplication | HIGH |
| **Fortune50 Skills** | 18 | ✓ Well-structured | None | - |
| **Enterprise Skills** | 2 | Duplicate | Overlap with fortune50-marketing & fortune50-sales | MEDIUM |
| **Utility Skills** | 5 | Mixed | seo-analytics-auditor & tech-debt-analyzer may be unused | LOW |
| **Special Folders** | 2 | Problem | document-skills (2.9MB bloat); _shared (reference only) | HIGH |
| **Documentation** | 3 README files | Out of sync | References non-existent "example-*" skills | MEDIUM |

---

## RECOMMENDATIONS FOR CONSOLIDATION & CLEANUP

### IMMEDIATE ACTIONS (High Priority)

1. **Delete `document-skills/` folder entirely**
   - Bloats the library by 2.9MB (62% of total)
   - Never used by Claude
   - Contains outdated OOXML schemas
   - Use specialized document tools if needed later

2. **Consolidate generic-* skills from 16 down to 4**
   - Keep only: `generic-code-reviewer`, `generic-design-system`, `generic-feature-developer`, `generic-ux-designer`
   - Delete all `generic-react-*`, `generic-fullstack-*`, `generic-static-*` (12 folders)
   - These can be made project-specific CLAUDE.md instructions instead
   - Rationale: 95% identical content, just different context. Better handled by project-level rules.

3. **Merge enterprise-* into fortune50-*:**
   - Move `enterprise-marketing-strategy` content into `fortune50-marketing`
   - Move `enterprise-sales-excellence` content into `fortune50-sales`
   - Delete the enterprise-* folders
   - Result: Consolidate 2 large skills into 2 Fortune 50 skills

### MEDIUM PRIORITY

4. **Rename "generic-*" prefix to match intent**
   - Current names are misleading
   - Recommend: Keep core 4 as `generic-*`
   - Consider naming convention review

5. **Update all README files**

   - Clarify relationship between enterprise-* and fortune50-* (after consolidation)

### OPTIONAL (Low Priority)

   - Or create clear "references/" convention
   - Currently unclear when to use these shared standards

8. **Add usage analytics**
   - Track which skills are invoked in prompts

   - Inform future consolidation decisions
